author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
ore mana! kena hena bujha viparīta
daṇḍe pale āyukṣaya, tāte tora bodha naya,
āise dina ithe haraṣita

dine māse avde bāḍha, aiche jāniyācha daḍha,
ghāṭe ke, tā bujhite nā pāra
nāye caḍi’ cāha kule, dekha jena pṛthvī cale,
tumi je calicha tā nā hera

dhana jana āpanāra, se nā bhāviyācha sāra,
se ki tora, jāna nā se kāra
tileke kāḍiyā laya, jāre icchā tāre deya,
nahe tumi marileo tāra

vṛthā ahaṅkāre mara, vicāriyā pūrvāpara,
sādhujana⋅pathete dā̐ḍāo
manuṣya durlabha janma, kena kara apakarma,
kare ratna pāiyā phelāo

jāvata sāmarthya āche, jarā nā āsiche kāche,
‘kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa’ kaha avirāma
jarāya bhāṅgibe tanu, sarvendriya habe kṣīṇu,
tabe ki sphuribe kṛṣṇa-nāma

nahe vā kakhane jāi, kichu nirūpaṇa nāi,
tile eka nāhika viśvāsa
premānanda kahe bhāi, kaha ‘kṛṣṇa’, byāja nāi,
e jīvana kevala niśvāsa
en_translation:
O my mind! Why do you understand things so incorrectly? Moment by moment, your life is declining, but you do not perceive it; instead, you are happy as the days pass by.

You firmly believe that the days, months, and years are increasing for you, but you do not understand who is truly diminishing. Sitting in a boat, you look at the shore and think the earth is moving, but you do not see that it is actually you who are moving.

You consider your wealth and family to be your own and essential to you. But are they truly yours? You do not even know to whom they really belong. In a moment they can be taken away and given to someone else; even after you die, they will not belong to you.

You are dying in vain pride—consider your past and future and stand on the path of the saintly. This human birth is rare; why do you commit sinful acts? It is like throwing away a jewel after finally finding it in your hand.

As long as you have strength and old age has not yet approached, chant "Kṛṣṇa! Kṛṣṇa!" incessantly. In old age, your body will break and all your senses will weaken; will the Name of Kṛṣṇa then appear in your mind?

No one knows when death will come; there is no certainty at all. There is not a single moment of assurance. Premānanda says: "O brother! Chant 'Kṛṣṇa!' without any delay, for this life is merely a single breath."
